title: Evidence Registry v1
description: Canonical evidence storage, naming, hashing, and lookup protocol. All verification evidence flows through this registry.

evidence_types.description: All recognized evidence types and their storage requirements.

diff.description: Code diff produced by a build action.

command_log.description: Shell command execution log with exit codes.

api_trace.description: HTTP request/response capture for API verification.

source_references.description: Source URLs and citations for research claims.

artifact_hash.description: Hash record proving immutability of another artifact.

visual_diff.description: Visual comparison between expected and actual screenshots.

verification_report.description: Evidence produced by verifier plugins during execution.

evidence_id_generation.description: How evidence IDs are generated to ensure uniqueness and traceability.

components.correlation_id_short: First 8 characters of the workflow correlation_id.
components.sequence: Zero-padded 4-digit sequence number within the workflow. Monotonically increasing.
components.type_short: Abbreviated evidence type (diff, tlog, clog, atrc, scrn, rrpt, sref, flog, trns, ahsh, vdif, vrpt).

evidence_id_generation.uniqueness_guarantee: Combination of correlation_id_short + sequence is unique within a workflow. Full evidence_id is globally unique when combined with workflow context.
evidence_id_generation.collision_handling: If collision detected, increment sequence. Registry rejects duplicate evidence_ids.

canonical_path_format.description: Deterministic file path for evidence storage.

components.correlation_id: Full UUID of the workflow correlation_id.
components.extension: File extension based on evidence type.

rules: All evidence for a workflow is co-located under the correlation_id directory.
No special characters except hyphens and dots.
Paths are relative to the evidence storage root.

hash_points.description: When and how evidence is hashed to prove integrity.

hash_points.when: Evidence artifact is first persisted to the registry.
hash_points.what: Full content of the evidence file.

hash_points.when: Evidence is referenced by a VerificationArtifact.
hash_points.what: Re-hash the evidence content and compare to stored hash.
hash_points.action_on_mismatch: Set criteria status to blocked. Evidence tampered or corrupted.

hash_points.what: Hash the entire VerificationArtifact (excluding artifact_hash field).

evidence_record.description: Metadata record stored in the registry for each piece of evidence.

required_fields.description: One of the recognized evidence types.
SHA-256 hash of the evidence content at time of registration.
Role or plugin that produced this evidence.
Size of the evidence file in bytes.

optional_fields.description: Criteria this evidence is associated with.
Build step that produced this evidence.

lookup_protocol.description: How the engine and plugins resolve evidence IDs to actual content.

operations.on_not_found: Return null. Engine sets criteria to blocked.

operations.on_partial_failure: Return found records + list of missing IDs.

operations.action: Re-hash content and compare to stored content_hash.

operations.constraints: Only Builder role and verifier plugins may register evidence.

retention_policy.description: Rules for evidence lifecycle and cleanup.

rules.rule: Evidence for completed workflows is retained for the retention_period.
rules.after_expiry: Evidence may be archived or deleted.

rules.rule: Evidence for active workflows is never deleted.
Evidence referenced by a VerificationArtifact cannot be deleted while the artifact exists.
Binary evidence (screenshots) may be compressed after workflow completion.
rules.constraint: Compressed content must hash-match the original (compression is lossless or original hash is preserved in metadata).

immutability_rules.rule: Once registered, evidence content cannot be modified. Any modification attempt is rejected.
Evidence metadata (EvidenceRecord) is append-only. Fields cannot be removed or changed after registration.
evidence_id is permanently bound to its content_hash. Re-registration with a different hash is rejected.

registry_invariants.rule: Every evidence_id in any BuildReport.evidence_map must resolve in the registry.
Every evidence_id in any VerificationArtifact.criteria_results must resolve in the registry.
The registry is the single source of truth for evidence. No out-of-band evidence references are valid.
Evidence types in the registry must be a superset of required_evidence_types from workflow_taxonomy.
